metadata.author: Use-case Reverse Engineering - Bialetti Gioia (v2 Rubin Ronchieri Byrd
metadata.product_name: Bialetti Gioia CF90 (domestic capsule espresso machine)
metadata.description: Disassembly tree v2.1 for the Bialetti Gioia CF90 capsule machine. Mass-balanced: every diamond conserves mass (input composite = extracted leaves + reduced composite), and the sum of all leaves equals the 2500 g root. Adds the user-accessible parts step (water tank, capsule drawer, capsule lever) and explicit thermoblock fasteners leaf. Action circles form sequential chains under each diamond. No routine-use operations modelled.

shapes.name: Bialetti Gioia (capsule espresso machine)
shapes.description: Domestic capsule espresso machine, Monosystem (Bialetti aluminium capsules only). 220-240 V, 1200 W. Thermoblock heating, vibration pump at 20 bar, 0.5 L removable water tank, capsule drawer for 7-8 used capsules. Approx. dimensions 93x340x221 mm, weight ~2.5 kg.

shapes.name: Open outer shell from chassis
shapes.tools: Torx T10, Phillips PH1, plastic pry tool

shapes.step_description: Place the machine upside-down on a soft cloth so the bottom plate is accessible; remove any residual water from the tank seat to avoid leaks.
Unscrew the 4 Torx T10 screws located at the corners of the bottom plate.
Unscrew the 2 Phillips PH1 screws behind the rear panel, near the power-cord grommet.
Release the 4 internal plastic clips along the vertical edges by gently prying with a flat plastic tool.
Lift the upper shell vertically, keeping it parallel to the base, to separate it from the internal chassis.

shapes.name: Outer plastic shell (top + sides + rear panel)
shapes.description: Single-piece outer casing after de-clipping, including top cover, left and right side walls and rear panel. On the 'Responsible' variant, manufactured with 50% post-consumer recycled (PCR) ABS.
shapes.component_name: Outer plastic shell (top + sides + rear panel)

shapes.name: Internal chassis assembly (full, after shell removal)
shapes.description: Internal frame still containing the front control panel, the hydraulic circuit, the main PCB with the wiring harness, the power cord, and the thermoblock assembly. Intermediate node: continues the disassembly chain.
shapes.component_name: Internal chassis assembly (full, after shell removal)

shapes.name: Disconnect and remove the front control-panel assembly

shapes.step_description: Unplug the JST-XH 4-pin connector that links the front button board to the main PCB; press the latch before pulling.
Unplug the JST-PH 2-pin connector of the aluminium-capsule sensor, located just behind the capsule lever assembly.
Unscrew the 2 Phillips PH0 screws that hold the button panel to the front of the chassis.
Slide the button panel forward and lift it out together with the integrated LED indicators and the capsule sensor.

shapes.name: Front button panel with LED indicators
shapes.description: Small PCB with 2 tactile buttons (espresso / long coffee) and status LEDs (power, water low, descaling, capsule-drawer full). Connected to the main PCB via a 4-pin JST-XH cable.
shapes.material_id: FR-4 PCB + tactile switches + LEDs + ABS cover
shapes.component_name: Front button panel with LED indicators

shapes.name: Aluminium-capsule recognition sensor
shapes.description: Inductive sensor that detects the metallic body of a genuine Bialetti aluminium capsule. Mounted near the capsule lever; feeds the main PCB with a digital presence signal.
shapes.material_id: Copper coil + ferrite core + plastic housing

shapes.name: Internal chassis (no front panel)
shapes.description: Internal frame after the front control-panel assembly has been removed. Still contains the hydraulic circuit, the main PCB with harness, the power cord, and the thermoblock assembly.

shapes.name: Extract pump, tubing and flow meter together
shapes.tools: Hose clamp pliers, Phillips PH1, container for residual water

shapes.step_description: Place a container under the chassis to catch ~30 mL of residual water that will drip from the tubing during removal.
Release the spring clamp on the silicone tube from the tank valve to the pump inlet; slide the tube off the barb.
Release the spring clamps on the silicone tubes from the pump outlet to the flow meter, and from the flow meter to the thermoblock inlet.
Unplug the 2-pin power connector that supplies the pump from the main PCB.
Unscrew the 2 Phillips PH1 screws holding the pump bracket to the chassis, then lift the pump together with the flow meter and the tubing as a single hydraulic assembly.

shapes.name: Vibration pump (Ulka EX5 type, 230 V, 48 W)
shapes.description: Solenoid vibration pump providing the nominal 20 bar working pressure (15 bar at the brew chamber after pressure drops). Driven directly from mains via the main PCB's triac. Copper coil, steel piston and rubber dampers.
shapes.material_id: Steel body, copper winding, brass fittings, rubber dampers
shapes.component_name: Vibration pump (Ulka EX5 type, 230 V, 48 W)

shapes.name: Silicone tubing set (tank-to-pump, pump-to-thermoblock)
shapes.description: Set of food-grade silicone tubes with internal diameter ~4 mm and spring clamps. Inlet tube from the tank valve, high-pressure tube to the thermoblock, short jumpers.
shapes.material_id: Food-grade silicone + zinc-plated steel spring clamps

shapes.name: Hall-effect flow meter
shapes.description: Small inline Hall-effect flow meter, used by the main PCB to measure delivered water volume; enables the programmable dose feature documented in the user manual.
shapes.material_id: ABS housing + ceramic shaft + Hall sensor PCB

shapes.name: Internal chassis (no panel, no hydraulic)
shapes.description: Internal frame after the front panel and the hydraulic circuit have been removed. Still contains the main PCB with harness, the power cord and the thermoblock assembly.

shapes.name: Extract main PCB, wiring harness and power cord
shapes.tools: Phillips PH0, JST connector tool, side cutters

shapes.step_description: Unplug all remaining JST connectors from the main PCB: thermostat lead, thermoblock heater lead, pump triac lead.
Disconnect the mains input wires from the PCB screw terminals (LIVE / NEUTRAL / EARTH).
Cut any cable ties that fix the wiring harness to the chassis ribs.
Unscrew the 4 Phillips PH0 screws securing the PCB to its plastic tray and lift the board out.
Pull the wiring harness and the power cord through the chassis cut-out as a single bundle.

shapes.name: Main control PCB
shapes.description: Single-sided FR-4 board with microcontroller, triacs driving the pump and heating element, mains rectifier and low-voltage regulator. Implements the programmable-dose logic.
shapes.material_id: FR-4 PCB + SMD components + electrolytic capacitors + triacs

shapes.name: Internal wiring harness
shapes.description: Internal cables linking the main PCB to the pump, thermoblock, thermostat, button board and sensor. 0.5-0.75 mm2 PVC-insulated copper wires and JST-XH/PH connectors.
shapes.material_id: Copper conductors + PVC insulation + nylon JST housings

shapes.name: Power cord with Schuko plug
shapes.description: Detachable mains cord, ~0.8 m long, CEE 7/7 (Schuko) plug, crimped ring/fork terminals on the PCB side.
shapes.material_id: 3-core copper cable + PVC jacket + thermoplastic Schuko plug

shapes.name: Internal chassis (only thermoblock left)
shapes.description: Bare metal chassis frame still hosting the thermoblock assembly in its bracket. All other components have been removed.

shapes.name: Extract the thermoblock assembly and free the chassis frame
shapes.tools: 10 mm wrench, Phillips PH1, heat-resistant gloves

shapes.step_description: Verify the machine has been unplugged for at least 30 minutes so the thermoblock has fully cooled before touching the metal body.
Disconnect the two faston terminals of the heating element from the harness, and the 2-pin JST of the safety thermostat.
Unscrew the brass nut at the thermoblock outlet (towards the brew chamber) using a 10 mm wrench; keep a cloth nearby for residual drips.
Unscrew the 2 Phillips PH1 screws of the thermoblock bracket on the chassis side wall.
Lift the thermoblock assembly vertically out of its seat.

shapes.name: Thermoblock assembly (heater + thermostat + body)
shapes.description: Heating subassembly used in place of a traditional boiler. Hot water on demand: cold water flows through a metal block heated by an internal resistive element; reaches brew temperature in seconds.

shapes.name: Bare chassis frame (terminal)
shapes.description: Empty internal frame remaining after all components have been removed. Steel structural elements with plastic brackets and rubber feet.
shapes.material_id: Galvanized steel + ABS brackets + EPDM feet

shapes.name: Mechanically disassemble the thermoblock into its leaves
shapes.tools: Bench vice, hex key, soft mallet

shapes.step_description: Clamp the aluminium body in a bench vice with soft jaws to avoid marking the surface; keep the heating-element side accessible.
Unscrew the safety thermostat from its threaded seat on the body; it comes out as a single threaded brass capsule.
Push out the heating element from its dedicated tunnel inside the aluminium body, gently tapping with a soft mallet if needed.
Separate the stainless-steel tube (water path) from the aluminium body; depending on the design, this may require a press or destructive cut.

shapes.name: Resistive heating element (1200 W)
shapes.description: Nichrome resistive wire on a ceramic carrier inside a metal sheath. Rated 1200 W at 230 V. Inserted in the aluminium body's tunnel. Faston-terminated.
shapes.material_id: Nichrome (NiCr 80/20) wire + ceramic carrier + stainless sheath

shapes.name: Bimetal safety thermostat
shapes.description: Bimetallic thermostat that opens the heating circuit if the thermoblock exceeds the safety threshold (~165 degC). Threaded brass body.
shapes.material_id: Brass body + bimetal disc + ceramic insulator

shapes.name: Cast aluminium thermoblock body
shapes.description: Pressure-cast aluminium body hosting the heating-element tunnel and the water-path channel. Acts as thermal mass. Key CRM-relevant component (aluminium recovery).

shapes.name: Stainless-steel water tube
shapes.description: Thin-wall stainless-steel tube embedded in the aluminium body, carrying water along the heated path. Food-safe contact surface. Often unrecoverable without destructive separation.

shapes.step_description: Lift out the 0.5 L removable water tank from its seat at the rear of the machine.
Pull out the used-capsule drawer from the front-bottom slot.
Release the 2 Phillips PH1 screws of the capsule lever / brewing-group assembly and lift it out.

shapes.name: Removable water tank (0.5 L)
shapes.description: Translucent removable water reservoir, 0.5 L nominal capacity, with bottom outlet valve feeding the pump. Snap-fit into the rear seat of the chassis.

shapes.name: Used-capsule drawer
shapes.description: Front-bottom drawer collecting 7-8 spent aluminium capsules, with drip tray and grid.

shapes.name: Capsule lever / brewing-group assembly
shapes.description: Lever mechanism that pierces and holds the capsule during brewing, including the brewing chamber and outlet spout.
shapes.material_id: Glass-filled nylon + stainless piercing plate + silicone seals

shapes.name: Internal chassis (no user-accessible parts)
shapes.description: Internal frame after the user-removable parts (water tank, capsule drawer, capsule lever/brewing group) have been taken off. Still contains the front control panel, hydraulic circuit, main PCB with harness, power cord and thermoblock.

shapes.name: Thermoblock fasteners and gaskets
shapes.description: Set of screws, brackets and high-temperature gaskets holding the heating element, thermostat and aluminium body together.
